titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Wolf Mankowitz Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 23:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, personal papers, printed material, audiovisual and computer media, and other papers documenting the career of British author, playwright, and screenwriter Wolf Mankowitz.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 107-114 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 115 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Fishburn Books on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Writings, 1944-2016, undated. II. Correspondence, 1947-2011. III. Personal Papers, 1919-2017, undated. IV. Printed Material, 1947-2007, undated. V. Audiovisual and Computer Media, 1960-2009, undated.

bioghist.head: Wolf Mankowitz (1924-1988)
bioghist.p: Cyril Wolf Mankowitz was a British author, playwright, and screenwriter. He was born in 1924 in London to Rebecca and Solomon Mankowitz and educated at Downing College (University of Cambridge), where one of his tutors was F. R. Leavis. Before beginning his career as an author and playwright, Mankowitz developed a reputation as an authority on and dealer of antique porcelain, leading to the publication of two reference books on Wedgwood.

Mankowitz's novel A Kid for Two Farthings (1953) was adapted into a film, for which he wrote the screenplay, in 1955. He also wrote the screenplay for The Millionairess (1960), which was nominated for a BAFTA award, and he was one of the screenwriters on the James Bond films Dr. No (1962) and Casino Royale (1967). Mankowitz's other novels include Make Me an Offer (1952) and My Old Man's a Dustman (1956). In 1958, he wrote the book for the musical Expresso Bongo , as well as the television script for East End, West End . His plays include The Bespoke Overcoat (1953), The Irish Hebrew Lesson (1978, 1980), and The Samson Riddle (1972, 1978). Mankowitz also published biographies of Edgar Allen Poe and Charles Dickens. His shorter works appeared in titles such as Good Housekeeping , Playboy , and the Evening Standard . In addition to writing, Mankowitz appeared on television programs and discussion panels on literature and the arts in the 1950s and 1960s.

Mankowitz married psychoanalyst Ann Seligmann in 1944, and they had four sons including Gered, a photographer best known for his portraits of Jimi Hendrix and the Rolling Stones. He moved to Ireland in 1971 and was made honorary consul to the Republic of Panama in Dublin the same year. Mankowitz died in Cork, Ireland in 1988.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Wolf Mankowitz Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, personal papers, printed material, audiovisual and computer media, and other papers documenting the career of author, playwright, and screenwriter Wolf Mankowitz. Writings contain drafts of novels, scripts for film, theater, and television projects, and articles and essays. Included are materials relating to Dickens of London , Expresso Bongo , A Kid For Two Farthings , Make Me an Offer , The Millionairess , and My Old Man's a Dustman . Among the correspondents are Lucille Lortel, Lord Beaverbrook (Max Aitken), Art Buchwald, Richard Burton, Peter Cook, Fanny E. Holtzmann, Cecil King, Cecil Madden, Sue Mengers, John Mills, and Laurence Olivier. Personal papers contain biographies, family material, legal documents, photographs, planners and diaries, and scrapbooks relating to works written or adapted for the stage by Mankowitz. Printed material consists of clippings and posters. Also included are film and audio recordings of plays.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Wolf Mankowitz Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of books, playscripts, screenplays, television scripts, short stories, poetry, and articles by Wolf Mankowitz. Included are drafts of his novels Make Me an Offer , A Kid for Two Farthings , and My Old Man's a Dustman , his reference works on Wedgwood, drafts of the musical Expresso Bongo , screenplays for The Millionairess and Black Beauty , and scripts for the television program East End, West End . Files often contain correspondence, research material, photographs, ephemera, notes, and publicity materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in three subseries: Books; Theater, Film, and Television; and Shorter Works.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings can also be found in Series II. Correspondence and Series III. Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence with editors, agents, actors, fellow writers, and other associates, primarily regarding professional matters, such as production of plays and publication of works by Wolf Mankowitz. Among the correspondents are Chesney Allen, Leslie Bricusse, Betty E. Box, Nat Cohen, Barry Cryer, Everett Greenbaum, Hugh Hefner, Sterling Lord, Lucille Lortel, Cecil Madden, Cyril Ornadel, Harold Prince, Bertrand Russell, and Norman Thaddeus Vane. The series also contains correspondence described by Mankowitz as "important letters" from public figures including Lord Beaverbrook (Max Aitken), Art Buchwald, Richard Burton, Michael Caine, Peter Cook, Frank Cousins, Milton H. Greene, Fanny E. Holtzmann, Cecil King, Frank Loesser, Sue Mengers, John Mills, Anthony Newley, Nanette Newman, Laurence Olivier, and Kenneth Tynan. Files sometimes include writings and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title, with first name only letters filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series I. Writings and Series II. Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains biographical information, personal financial and legal records, agreements and related material for professional projects, ephemera, photographs, planners and diaries, and scrapbooks documenting books, films, and plays by Mankowitz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of clippings and serials containing articles by or about Wolf Mankowitz, a small amount of ephemera related to his work, catalogs and booklets, and posters advertising plays and musicals written or produced by Mankowitz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes of audiotapes, videotapes, long-playing records, and floppy disks by or relating to Wolf Mankowitz and stage productions of Expresso Bongo , Belle; or the Ballad of Dr. Crippen , The Hebrew Lesson , and My Old Man's a Dustman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two subseries: Audiovisual Media and Computer Media.
